programmes.show_description: How do they forge super steel to make a car lighter, greener and safer? And, how do they shear Australia's eighty million sheep every Spring? S11, Ep15
How do they keep you comfortable, entertained and safe with the latest aircraft seats? And, how do they design bras with aerospace technology to give you that extra lift? S11, Ep16
Range Rover. Mike buys a bargain Range Rover P38 and Edd sets about mending it. They head to the wilds of Exmoor to give the car a final test drive before searching for a buyer. S8, Ep3
Frogeye Sprite. Mike attempts to double his money by turning an old Frogeye Sprite into a premiership quality classic car to be test driven around the Brooklands Motor Museum. S8, Ep4
Ray Day. Henry and Sam visit a shop hoping to sell a bike and get side-tracked by the wares on offer. Will they manage to find some good bargains amongst all the delights? S2, Ep24
Lancashire Hotpot. Henry and Sam are near Preston, visiting a collector of military gear and Americana. Much to their surprise they spot something a little unusual. S2, Ep25
Decorative mouldings, commercial pulleys and hoses are put under the microscope to find out how they were made. S2, Ep11
Putty knives, garage doors, electric motors and wool. More everyday objects are examined as we find out how they were made. S2, Ep12
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Brenton uses electricity to restore the shine on a precious trophy. And, four experts pool their talents to repair a battered old ventriloquist's dummy. S4, Ep21
Changing With The Times. Drew and Al buy from businesses in the process of change. They find a striking Windsor chair, an anatomical model, and artefacts from a former mill. S19, Ep6
Talking Shop. With her new shop opening, Viki fills the van while on the road with Drew. They snap up French advertising art and a dolls-house cabinet. S19, Ep7
The Poseidon Crew receive devastating news about their mining permits. The Dust Devils gamble big on a quartz reef. Shane and Russell strike gold. S5, Ep5
A new crew are under threat from crocodiles and accusations of illegal prospecting. The Victoria Diggers receive some welcome help. S5, Ep6
Raj Bisram and Paul Martin reach Shakespeare country in Warwickshire: to buy or not to buy? Plus, they meet alpacas and do some ballet. S22, Ep15
Angus heads to Bradford to look through a vast collection of coins, toys and jewellery. Plus, the team clear Yorkshire-made furniture from a beautiful cottage. S2, Ep5
Brenton uses electricity to restore the shine on a precious trophy. And, four experts pool their talents to repair a battered old ventriloquist's dummy. S4, Ep21
Pump Action. In Lancashire, Henry and Sam explore a garage packed with hundreds of treasures and trinkets. They encounter various military vehicles and a lovely old petrol pump. S2, Ep26
A Hard Bargain. Henry and Sam are across the sea in Northern Ireland, searching for collections they've never seen before. Will they find any unusual or unique items? S2, Ep27
Rob Tilley. Henry and Fuzz visit Rob in Shropshire. They uncover a Matchless G3, tin signs, and a BSA mower in a shed that is Henry's idea of heaven. S7, Ep9
Stuart Mills. In a shed in Nottinghamshire, Henry and Fuzz find a rugged Ural sidecar. Built for war and wilderness, can they revive it, or will it win the battle? S7, Ep10
Brian enlists Angus to help his singer daughter Lucy raise money for a mixing desk. And Angus visits Edwina's home to help raise funds for Ukraine. S4, Ep1
Angus helps comic Tommy sell his collection of showbiz memorabilia. Then Vanessa needs him to free up some space in her incredible north London home. S4, Ep2
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep24
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep25
David Kingerley. Henry and Fuzz visit David's Derbyshire shed, a treasure trove of vintage mechanical gems, and uncover a wonderful Monet-Goyon motorbike. S7, Ep11
Richard Gaunt. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's Staffordshire shed, uncovering an Excelsior and Mobylette as they dive into a treasure trove of two-wheeled gems. S7, Ep12
Audio expert Mark restores a vintage jukebox that holds fond memories for widower Jeff. Plus, silversmith Brenton navigates his way through an old compass. S5, Ep41
Musical expert David attempts to bring a beloved pump organ back to life. Meanwhile, furniture restorer Will works on a handmade crib from World War I. S5, Ep42
How is plasterboard made from power station smoke? How do they cast the world's top awards? How do they build a plane that can take off and land almost anywhere? S11, Ep17
How do they make car windows to keep out wind and rain, stones and thieves, and even noise and harmful UV rays? And, how do they put the gloss on lipstick? S11, Ep18
How do they make George Washington's favourite tipple? How do they crash test aircraft seats without crashing a plane? How do they produce billions of batteries a year? S11, Ep19
How do they mine nickel to turn into nickels? How do they build the lock gates for canals to allow barges to climb hills? How do they put the bubbles in bubble tea? S11, Ep20
Saab 9-3. Mike wants a high performance car with an historic badge, but it's hard to find a Saab 9-3 Turbo at a good price. With tight margins will Edd have any room for manoeuvre? S8, Ep5
Dodge Charger. Mike heads to America in search of one of the finest cars in the US, the Dodge Charger. But such a prestigious car carries a hefty price tag. Can he make a profit? S8, Ep6
Pump Action. In Lancashire, Henry and Sam explore a garage packed with hundreds of treasures and trinkets. They encounter various military vehicles and a lovely old petrol pump. S2, Ep26
A Hard Bargain. Henry and Sam are across the sea in Northern Ireland, searching for collections they've never seen before. Will they find any unusual or unique items? S2, Ep27
Toothpicks, acrylic bathtubs, helicopters and beer. More objects are put under the microscope as we find out how they were made. S2, Ep13
Nails, staples, safety glasses, fabrics and bicycles are put under the microscope to find out how they were made. S2, Ep14
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Wayne Meets Triplets. Wayne gets a lead on a cache of three 308 Ferraris in a nearby garage. Then, Roger works on a belt job while Wayne puts on his sales hat to drum up clients. S6, Ep21
Big Brass. Wayne gets a lead on a collection of his newest obsession, Brass cars. There's a 1913 Hudson, a 1910 Stoddard-Dayton, a 1906 Oldsmobile and a Redding motorcycle. S6, Ep22
Wayne Goes Skiing. Wayne helps Olympic gold medallist Jonny Moseley and the Squaw Valley ski resort find a vehicle that could have been at the 1960 Winter Olympics. S6, Ep23
Kiss a Few Frogs. Wayne finds a rare 1934 Lincoln KB Convertible. But to buy it, he must meet a high price and buy two other cars - a '70s Chrysler K Car and '60s Buick convertible. S6, Ep24
VW 181 Thing. Mike sources a VW 181 Thing from a collector, but is forced to dig deep for the purchase, meaning there isn't much left in the kitty for Edd to refurbish it. S12, Ep7
VW Corrado VR6. Mike is ecstatic when he snaps up a cult VW Corrado coupe with only one previous owner for a bargain price. But with cheap cars come expensive surprises... S12, Ep17
VW Split-Screen. Mike wants to splash the cash on a custom bus but Edd favours a more conservative campervan. Edd is an air-cooled expert but will this strategy pay dividends? S11, Ep11
VW Beetle - Part 1. Auto expert Mike Brewer and mechanical maestro Edd China get under the bonnet of a VW Beetle, but will they be able to breathe life into this ancient wonder? S6, Ep7
VW Beetle - Part 2. Mechanical duo Mike and Edd are working on a VW Beetle which begins to take shape. But it's not all smooth cruising for the team in this topsy-turvy restoration. S6, Ep8
VW T2 Panel Van. Mike hunts out a classic camper but impossibly high prices force him to settle for a less popular panel model. Can Edd realise the ambitious plans for its upgrade? S7, Ep3
1970 VW Fastback. Mike finds a 1970 VW Flashback that needs some love and attention from Ant to be roadworthy again. If they can finish the upgrade on time, will they make a profit? S15, Ep11
VW Corrado V2. Mike finds a 1992 Corrado G60 smothered in aftermarket mods. Parts are rare and superchargers can be costly, but Elvis determines to get this risky project done. S18, Ep10
Hot Rod. In Northamptonshire, Henry and Fuzz dive into an eclectic mix of British and American vehicles. Henry and Fuzz end up smitten with a customised Hot Rod. S1, Ep5
The Daimler Ferret. Henry and Fuzz rummage through two huge sheds of vintage military vehicles. There are army quad bikes, amphibious troop carriers, and a Ferret Scout car. S1, Ep6
Dragstar. Henry and Fuzz stop by a shed in Leighton Buzzard filled with astonishing American muscle cars. The pair pick a trusty classic with room for a profit. S1, Ep7
Jaguar E-Type. Henry and Fuzz head up to Yorkshire, hoping to revisit 'the one that got away'. It's a rare E-type Jaguar Rat Rod with a huge surprise under the bonnet. S1, Ep8
Marshalling The Raw Recruits. New staff are thrown in the deep end on a rare Lotus, and a unique race car. Richard's beloved steam-car restoration threatens to run out of puff. S5, Ep7
1950 Austin Atlantic. Henry and Fuzz head to Bristol to see Robert's 1950 Austin Atlantic. Faded and forgotten for years, can they revive this bold British classic? S4, Ep1
MotoGP: USA Sprint Race. All the action from the third Sprint Race of the 2026 MotoGP World Championship, at the Circuit Of The Americas in the US, near Austin, Texas. S1, Ep3
Clocked & Loaded Auction. Angus helps a roofer-turned-artist sell his sculptures for a small fortune. Plus, a woman is auctioning off her inherited clock collection. S6, Ep4
The Final Countdown. The Misfits are left to fend for themselves as 'Opal' Joe Kalmar's health worsens. The Bushmen are desperate to salvage what's left of their season. S8, Ep20
